# Agent Bridge

Repository: https://github.com/creatornader/agent-bridge
Package: @creatornader/agent-bridge

Agent Bridge is a durable, pull-first mailbox and work-delivery control plane for AI agents in different clients, processes, sessions, and machines.
History uses mailbox `inbox` (default), `sent`, or `all`. Receipt state is `any` (default), `unread`, or `read`; it is caller-relative and inbox-only. CLI: `inbox`, `sent`, and `history --mailbox/--receipt-state`. Deprecated unacked-principal arguments must equal the configured identity.

## Install

- `npm install --global @creatornader/agent-bridge`
- `agent-bridge init --provider local`
- `agent-bridge demo`
- Development gateway: create `.secrets/postgres_password` and
  `.secrets/runtime_password`, then run `docker compose up --build --wait gateway`.
  Both checked-in port bindings are loopback-only. See `docs/deployment.md` before a
  non-loopback deployment.

If an MCP client cannot attach, run `agent-bridge doctor --json` and see
`docs/troubleshooting.md`. Normal client installation resolves the MCP launch command;
do not copy another runtime's identity or backend file.

The `codex` host adapter configures the profile shared by the Codex CLI and the Codex
surface in the ChatGPT desktop app. Claude Code and Claude Desktop use separate
adapters. OpenClaw and generic MCP manifests are operator-managed. Scripts and
services can call the CLI or HTTPS API or embed the Node library without a host adapter.

## Modes

- local: SQLite WAL, no account or daemon
- gateway: authenticated HTTPS, PostgreSQL authority, SQLite edge outbox and cache
- legacy-supabase: compatibility with the v1 shared_context schema

## Commands

- `agent-bridge --version` or `agent-bridge -V`: print the installed package version without loading backend configuration.
- `agent-bridge --help`, `agent-bridge -h`, or a command followed by either help flag: print help without loading backend configuration or dispatching the command.
- `agent-bridge capabilities`: print the canonical v2 operation and protocol capability document.

- agent-bridge init --provider local
- agent-bridge demo
- `agent-bridge status`: passive JSON snapshot; never probes or synchronizes. Unprobed remote reachability returns unknown with `connected: false`.
- `agent-bridge doctor`: active named checks; exits 0 ok, 2 degraded, 1 failed. Blocked outbox rows cannot be healthy.
- agent-bridge pending
- agent-bridge send, inbox, history, acknowledge
- agent-bridge claim [--message-id <uuid>], extend, ack, nack, deliveries, delivery-events, cancel, requeue
- agent-bridge sync, watch, join, presence
- agent-bridge clients install <runtime> --identity <principal>
- agent-bridge clients inspect <runtime> --identity <principal> --instance <stable-key> --backend-config <absolute path>
- agent-bridge clients adopt <runtime> --identity <principal> --instance <stable-key> --backend-config <absolute path> [--apply]
- agent-bridge clients repair <runtime> --identity <principal> --instance <stable-key> [--apply] [--resume <uuid>] [--recover-lock]
- agent-bridge clients update <runtime> --identity <principal> --instance <stable-key> [--command <command>] [--apply] [--resume <uuid>] [--recover-lock]
- agent-bridge clients uninstall <runtime> --identity <principal> --instance <stable-key> [--apply] [--resume <uuid>] [--recover-lock]
- agent-bridge clients rollback <update-operation-id> --identity <principal> [--apply] [--recover-lock]
- agent-bridge clients migrate stage <runtime> --identity <principal> --instance <stable-key> --enrollment-file <private-path> [--apply] [--recover-lock]
- agent-bridge clients migrate cutover <stage-operation-id> --exclusive-edge [--apply] [--recover-lock]
- agent-bridge clients migrate finalize <cutover-operation-id> --exclusive-edge [--apply] [--recover-lock]
- agent-bridge clients resume <operation-id> [--recover-lock]  # resumes recorded v3, supported v4, v5 stage, or v6 migration authority
- agent-bridge clients operations [<operation-id>]  # read-only resumable/classification-required/blocked/complete summaries
- agent-bridge owner provision|inventory|rotate|revoke
- agent-bridge clients install <runtime> --enrollment-file <private path>
- agent-bridge archive export|verify|import
- agent-bridge dr backup|verify|restore

Client inspection is local, read-only, secret-free, and reports absent, unmanaged,
managed, or drifted. Adoption is a plan unless `--apply` is explicit. It writes only
owner-private credential-free management metadata after the identity, stable instance,
backend path, scope, and launch contract match exactly; it never rewrites the host
registration or backend file. Enrollment-based first-time provisioning continues to
refuse registration and backend-file collisions. Metadata binds Desktop to its
normalized config path and Codex to its active profile config. Claude Code local and
project scope bind to the invocation directory because its native CLI exposes no
stronger registration target.
The backend and its immediate parent must pass the owner-only no-link policy. Codex
registration comparison ignores diagnostic fields and JSON property order but requires
exactly AGENT_BRIDGE_AGENT, AGENT_BRIDGE_INSTANCE, and AGENT_BRIDGE_CONFIG. Claude Code
connectivity status is not registration state. Desktop inspection uses the installer's
validated absolute launch resolver, and applied adoption re-inspects its postcondition.
Only Claude Code accepts `--scope`; only Claude Desktop accepts `--config-path` for
inspect and adopt. Repair, update, and uninstall derive those locators from strict managed metadata.
They reject backend, scope, and config-path authority flags. `--identity` binds the
metadata record and immutable request. Repair restores the recorded launch contract.
Update records a normalized credential-free launch request, including only fixed Agent
Bridge environment key names. Native commands are one executable contract and reject
arguments, URL-like selectors, and credential surfaces. Plan-only calls create no lock
or operation. Native changes prove remove then add. Desktop replaces only the Agent
Bridge config entry through a private operation-scoped temporary file. Its same-user
writer race is advisory because Node cannot provide an OS transaction. Resume requires
the same action and managed identity. Lock recovery requires
same-host process-death proof.

Archive export moves one workspace's immutable messages and read receipts between
canonical local SQLite and PostgreSQL stores. Import defaults to a rollback-backed dry
run and requires `--apply` to commit. PostgreSQL archive authority comes only from
`AGENT_BRIDGE_ARCHIVE_DATABASE_URL`. The digest detects changed bytes; it is neither
encryption nor authentication. Archives exclude delivery, event, presence, credential,
control, and security state. Import uses one private file descriptor for format,
message, and receipt passes with a 4 MiB batch budget. Export completes its audit only
after durable publication. `--dry-run` and `--apply` are exclusive. Library import:
`@creatornader/agent-bridge/archive`.
Portable v1 requires current-domain records, lowercase UUIDs, and six-digit UTC
timestamps. It does not trim, deduplicate, default, or rewrite message content.
Legacy or direct rows that fail validation require native database recovery.
Export accepts a request ID, stores it as header `exportRequestId`, and returns it.
Verify and import return the same provenance; an import request ID is independent.
Same-ID export retries verify completed files or reconcile matching started files
without streaming again. Deterministic adjacent recovery files are derived from the
export ID. Force replacement keeps a private backup until the new file is durable and
reports recovery paths plus backup, durability, and audit uncertainty.

Native DR preserves a complete local SQLite authority or shared PostgreSQL authority;
it does not translate between providers. Local DR rejects gateway edge stores.
PostgreSQL source and target URLs come only from AGENT_BRIDGE_DR_SOURCE_DATABASE_URL
and AGENT_BRIDGE_DR_TARGET_DATABASE_URL. Restore requires a dedicated fresh database
with the same name and PostgreSQL major, superuser authority, an explicit request ID,
and --accept-source-sql-risk. The backup includes role membership and default ACL state,
excludes transient request, rate-limit, instance, and archive-authorization rows, and
normalizes claimed deliveries on restore. Never run source and restored target at the
same time. The package API is @creatornader/agent-bridge/dr. Bundle hashes detect
changed bytes but do not encrypt or authenticate the source.
The immutable PostgreSQL gateway authority UUID is preserved by native DR. It identifies
one logical authority across restore but does not fence a live clone.

## MCP tools

- `capabilities`: return operations, required scopes, provider-specific enforcement, and supported protocol versions. Gateway access needs an active credential but no named scope for this operation.
- Production gateway capabilities report `requestAuthority: true` and `rowIsolation: true` only after the migration and live catalog, role, policy, privilege, and function checks pass. PostgreSQL forces RLS on messages, receipts, deliveries, delivery events, and presence. Lease transitions and target-to-delivery membership remain service-enforced.
- Authenticated gateway `/v2/status` returns optional `gatewayAuthorityId` and
  `credentialId` fields when PostgreSQL request authority is active. They are additive
  in HTTP 2.1 and do not appear in capabilities.

Compatibility: post_context, get_context, ack_context.
Reliable messaging: send, history, claim, extend, acknowledge, negative_acknowledge, heartbeat, presence, sync.
Claim selects the next due delivery unless HTTP 2.1, MCP, or CLI supplies one messageId. The exact form atomically selects and maintains only that eligible recipient delivery. HTTP 2.0 rejects the additive field.
Publisher delivery intent: `deliveryPolicy` mode `mailbox|leased` with `maxAttempts`, `retryBaseDelayMs`, `retryMaxDelayMs`, `retryJitterRatio`, and optional `notBefore`. MCP delivery controls: `list_deliveries`, `list_delivery_events`, `cancel_delivery`, `requeue_delivery`. Consumer `maxAttempts` and `retryPolicy` are validated but ignored for one compatibility release.

Gateway MCP clients sync automatically and expose manual sync for bounded outbox replay and inbox cache refresh. Offline unacknowledged results are degraded candidates with unknown acknowledgement state, not proof that messages are unread or that no remote work exists.

## Protocol boundary

A2A and application task semantics sit above Agent Bridge.
MCP, CLI, HTTPS, and the Node library are access surfaces.
Harnesses, host applications, host adapters, and access surfaces are separate layers.
The MCP server uses stdio. The gateway HTTPS API is not MCP over HTTP.
Optional transports such as SLIM or NATS may sit below the core.
agmsg is a reference for adapters, interoperability, and client experience.
HTTP protocol 2.1 is current. Upgraded gateways continue to serve released headerless and explicit 2.0 clients. New 2.1 clients probe before mutation and proceed only after complete, consistent negotiation selects and advertises 2.1. They reject headerless or selected 2.0 gateways instead of downgrading. Upgrade the gateway before 2.1 clients. Capabilities distinguish current, selected, and supported versions.
MCP and HTTP 2.1 use delivery response envelopes. The unversioned CLI and HTTP 2.0 retain direct or null delivery results.
OpenAPI paths describe 2.1. Embedded 2.0 vendor extensions provide limited compatibility schema metadata, not a second OpenAPI description.

## Acknowledgment rules

MCP ack_context and CLI acknowledge write read receipts.
MCP acknowledge and CLI ack settle claimed deliveries.
A receipt does not settle work. Delivery settlement does not create a receipt.
Lease extension does not report progress or external task completion.

## Identity rule

The active client supplies AGENT_BRIDGE_AGENT and may supply a stable AGENT_BRIDGE_INSTANCE consumer key. Do not hardcode another client name and do not store agent identity in the shared backend config. Gateway installations use a separate principal-bound token and owner-only backend file for each client. Supported installers generate and persist the instance key; direct clients may do so when they need separate consumer state or presence. The gateway does not bind it to an installer registration. It is not a PID, unique live process, session, or thread. Unless AGENT_BRIDGE_CURSOR is explicit, processes that share one key also share its cursor path. They also share delivery lease ownership and instance-keyed presence. Without a key or explicit cursor path, cursor storage uses `default`. Leases use the principal, and presence is unavailable.

Owner commands are offline and use only AGENT_BRIDGE_OPERATOR_DATABASE_URL. They are
not MCP, HTTP, or public gateway operations. Provision and rotation write private
versioned enrollment files before calling PostgreSQL. The installer consumes the raw
token from that file without command arguments. Rotation updates the exact existing
client backend and does not repeat host registration. Enrollment operations hold an
exclusive per-file lock and use revisioned compare-and-swap transitions. Use explicit
`--recover-lock` only after a crash; recovery checks lock age, host, owner, and process
liveness. Rotation requires predecessor credential, principal, instance, gateway,
workspace, and live MCP registration matches. Consumed-file deletion and lock release
report retained, missing, durable, and durability-unknown outcomes without claiming that
an unlinked path was retained.

`clients migrate stage` is a plan-first gateway migration preparation command. An
applied stage creates an owner-private successor backend and credential-free operation
record. It leaves the active registration, active backend, and source edge scope
active after initializing its gate. Staging requires an absolute, normalized source
edge database path and rejects `:memory:`.

`clients migrate cutover` requires a committed v5 stage and `--exclusive-edge`. Initial
preflight checks exact HTTP 2.1 status bindings at both gateways, one immutable
authority ID, the successor's `status:read` and `messages:write` grants, and a direct
predecessor-to-successor route challenge. Once it creates the journal, it uses a
retained normalized source URL and the successor credential for every route proof and
source outbox replay through the target gateway. `finalize` requires a fresh assertion
after grace expires and retires the source. To return to a former endpoint, rotate a
new owner credential and run an ordinary forward cutover to that endpoint. Dry runs
read private edge state without SQLite writes or gateway requests. They refuse live WAL
sidecars rather than read uncheckpointed outbox state. Resume uses only the retained v6
request and waits for an unexpired drain lease instead of taking it over.

Project is an optional immutable message label, not a tenant or identity selector. Omitted project reads include labeled and unlabeled messages. Exact project filters are supported by CLI, MCP, HTTP, and stores. Gateway workspace assertions must match the bound credential before network access.
Local CLI calls may override workspace for one invocation. The legacy Supabase schema is global and always reports workspace `*`; use project labels for legacy filtering.

## References

- README.md: setup, commands, security, and provider behavior
- ROADMAP.md: released, implemented, and remaining work
- SECURITY.md: reporting process and security boundaries
- docs/ecosystem.md: relationship to adjacent protocols, brokers, and runtimes
- docs/troubleshooting.md: MCP startup and client recovery
- docs/deployment.md: Compose development stack and production deployment constraints
- docs/decisions/0001-protocol-layers-and-acknowledgment-semantics.md: protocol layers and acknowledgment semantics
- docs/decisions/0002-canonical-operation-contract-registry.md: generated contracts and version negotiation
- docs/decisions/0003-host-adapters-and-consumer-instance-keys.md: host layers and instance-key semantics
- docs/architecture-v2.md: protocol, storage, identity, delivery, and acceptance decisions
- clients/*.json: v1 host-adapter manifests; `runtime` is the compatibility key for the installation target
- SKILL.md: concise agent operating instructions
